---
title: "Suaza — Lagged Effects: Education & Suicide Mortality"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Setup
```{r}
library(here)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(purrr)
```

## Load data
```{r}
df <- read_csv(here("outputs/csv/analytics_suaza.csv"))
#df <- read_csv(here("../../outputs/csv/analytics_suaza.csv"))
#glimpse(df)
```

## Visual inspection first

Before running correlations, plot both series together to get a feel for the data.
```{r}
df |>
  select(anio, valor, desercion) |>
  pivot_longer(-anio, names_to = "serie", values_to = "valor_serie") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = anio, y = valor_serie, color = serie)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point() +
  facet_wrap(~serie, scales = "free_y") +
  labs(title = "Suaza — Suicide mortality vs school dropout over time")
```
```{r}
df |>
  select(anio, valor, repitencia) |>
  pivot_longer(-anio, names_to = "serie", values_to = "valor_serie") |>
  group_by(serie) |>
  mutate(z = scale(valor_serie)[,1]) |>
  ggplot(aes(anio, z, color = serie)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point() +
  labs(
    title = "Suaza — Standardized trends: suicide mortality vs repitencia",
    y = "Standardized value (z-score)",
    x = "Year"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```

## Cross-correlation across multiple lags
```{r}
# Compute correlations for lags 0 through 5
suicide <- df$valor
dropout <- df$desercion
n <- length(suicide)

lags <- 0:5

correlations <- map_dfr(lags, function(lag) {
  if (lag == 0) {
    x <- dropout
    y <- suicide
  } else {
    x <- dropout[1:(n - lag)]
    y <- suicide[(lag + 1):n]
  }

  tibble(
    lag = lag,
    r_pearson  = cor(x, y, use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson"),
    r_spearman = cor(x, y, use = "complete.obs", method = "spearman"),
    n = sum(complete.cases(cbind(x, y)))
  )
})

correlations
```
```{r}
correlations |>
  ggplot(aes(x = lag, y = r_spearman)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#2c7bb6", width = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = lags) +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-correlation: dropout (t) → suicide mortality (t + lag)",
    x = "Lag (years)",
    y = "Spearman ρ"
  )
```

## Expand to all education indicators
```{r}
edu_vars <- c("desercion",
              "aprobacion", "reprobacion", "repitencia")

all_correlations <- map_dfr(edu_vars, function(var) {
  map_dfr(lags, function(lag) {
    x <- df[[var]]
    y <- df$valor

    if (lag > 0) {
      x <- x[1:(n - lag)]
      y <- y[(lag + 1):n]
    }

    tibble(
      indicator = var,
      lag = lag,
      r = cor(x, y, use = "complete.obs", method = "spearman"),
      n = sum(complete.cases(cbind(x, y)))
    )
  })
})

all_correlations
```
```{r}
all_correlations |>
  ggplot(aes(x = lag, y = r, fill = r > 0)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.6) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  facet_wrap(~indicator, ncol = 2) +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = lags) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("#d7191c", "#2c7bb6"), guide = "none") +
  labs(
    title = "Cross-correlation: education indicators → suicide mortality",
    subtitle = "Dropout, coverage, approval rates at lags 0–5 years",
    x = "Lag (years)",
    y = "Spearman ρ"
  )
```

## Regression with strongest lag

### Deserción
```{r}
lag_n <- 1

df_model <- df |>
  mutate(desercion_lagged = lag(desercion, lag_n)) |>
  filter(!is.na(desercion_lagged))

model <- lm(valor ~ desercion_lagged, data = df_model)
summary(model)
```
```{r}
df_model |>
  ggplot(aes(x = desercion_lagged, y = valor)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#2c7bb6") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Suicide mortality ~ dropout (lag ", lag_n, " years)"),
    x = paste0("Dropout rate (t - ", lag_n, ")"),
    y = "Suicide mortality (per 100k)"
  )
```

### Repitencia
```{r}
lag_n <- 5

df_model <- df |>
  mutate(repitencia_lagged = lag(repitencia, lag_n)) |>
  filter(!is.na(repitencia_lagged))

model <- lm(valor ~ repitencia_lagged, data = df_model)
summary(model)
```
```{r}
df_model |>
  ggplot(aes(x = repitencia_lagged, y = valor)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#2c7bb6") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Suicide mortality ~ repetition (lag ", lag_n, " years)"),
    x = paste0("Repetition rate (t - ", lag_n, ")"),
    y = "Suicide mortality (per 100k)"
  )
```

### Reprobación
```{r}
lag_n <- 5

df_model <- df |>
  mutate(reprobacion_lagged = lag(reprobacion, lag_n)) |>
  filter(!is.na(reprobacion_lagged))

model <- lm(valor ~ reprobacion_lagged, data = df_model)
summary(model)
```
```{r}
df_model |>
  ggplot(aes(x = reprobacion_lagged, y = valor)) +
  geom_point(size = 2) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#2c7bb6") +
  labs(
    title = paste0("Suicide mortality ~ failure (lag ", lag_n, " years)"),
    x = paste0("Failure rate (t - ", lag_n, ")"),
    y = "Suicide mortality (per 100k)"
  )
```

```{r}
pf <- read_csv(here("outputs/csv/suicide_huila.csv"))
pf |> distinct(territorio) |> arrange(territorio)
pf |>
  count(territorio, name = "n_anio") |>
  arrange(desc(n_anio))
pf |> distinct(sexo)
pf |> summarise(min_anio = min(anio), max_anio = max(anio))
edu <- read_csv(here("outputs/csv/huila_map.csv"))
glimpse(edu)
edu |> glimpse()
edu |> select(NAME_2, municipio) |> head(10)
```

```{r}
library(jsonlite)

# Quick check without downloading everything
url <- "https://www.datos.gov.co/resource/nudc-7mev.json?$where=departamento=%27Huila%27&$select=a_o,municipio&$limit=50000"
test <- fromJSON(url)

test |>
  mutate(anio = as.integer(a_o)) |>
  summarise(
    min_anio = min(anio, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_anio = max(anio, na.rm = TRUE),
    n_municipios = n_distinct(municipio)
  )
```

```{r}
suicide_municipios <- pf |>
  distinct(territorio) |>
  pull(territorio) |>
  sort()

edu_municipios <- test |>
  distinct(municipio) |>
  pull(municipio) |>
  sort()

# Which are in suicide but not education
setdiff(suicide_municipios, edu_municipios)

# Which are in education but not suicide
setdiff(edu_municipios, suicide_municipios)
```
